Precision Gauge Products

From standard plug gauges to highly specialized custom solutions, Merc Technologies manufactures precision gauges for dimensional inspection across all industries. Every gauge is manufactured to exacting tolerances and 100% inspected.

Standard Plain Gauges

Our standard plain gauge line covers the most common dimensional inspection needs. All gauges manufactured to ASME B89.1.5 standards with Class X, XX, or XXX tolerances.

Plain Plug Gauges

  • Sizes: 0.059" to 12.000"
  • GO/NO-GO or progressive styles
  • Tool steel or carbide
  • Tolerance classes: X, XX, XXX

Plain Ring Gauges

  • Sizes: 0.059" to 12.000"
  • GO/NO-GO sets available
  • Chrome plated or carbide faced
  • Tolerance classes: X, XX, XXX

Snap Gauges

  • Sizes: 0.100" to 12.000"
  • Adjustable or fixed anvils
  • Carbide contact points
  • Progressive or GO/NO-GO

Thread Gauges

Complete line of thread gauges for all common thread forms. Manufactured to exact specifications with full certification documentation.

Available Thread Forms

  • UN/UNF (Unified National)
  • UNJ (Unified National with controlled root radius)
  • Metric (ISO 68-1)
  • NPT/NPTF (National Pipe Thread)
  • ACME and Buttress
  • Custom thread forms

Specifications

  • Tolerance classes: X, XX, XXX
  • Pitch diameters per ASME B1.2
  • Major/minor diameter control
  • Thread angle verification
  • GO/NO-GO sets or single gauges
  • Full dimensional reports available

Thread Plug Gauges

Precision ground thread gauges for internal thread inspection

Thread Ring Gauges

External thread verification with full certification

Carbide Gauges

For high-volume production environments, carbide gauges offer superior wear resistance and extended service life. Our carbide gauges maintain accuracy through millions of inspection cycles.

Carbide Advantages

  • Extended Service Life: 10-20x longer than tool steel in high-volume applications
  • Dimensional Stability: Minimal wear ensures consistent measurements over time
  • Cost Effective: Lower cost-per-inspection in production environments
  • Size Range: Available from 0.010" to 12.000" diameter

Available Configurations

Carbide Plug Gauges

Solid carbide or carbide-tipped in all standard sizes

Carbide Ring Gauges

Carbide measuring surface with steel body

Carbide Snap Gauges

Carbide anvils for maximum wear resistance

Master Gauges & Setting Standards

Master gauges and setting standards for calibration, gauge verification, and measurement system analysis. All masters include NIST-traceable calibration certificates.

Setting Masters

Precision cylindrical masters for setting snap gauges, comparators, and bore gauges. Tolerance: ±0.000010" (±0.25 microns)

Calibration Standards

Reference standards for gauge calibration and verification per ISO 10012-1. Full traceability documentation included.

Thread Setting Plugs

Master thread plugs for setting thread ring gauges and verification. Class ZZZ tolerance for ultimate accuracy.

Special Purpose Gauges

Specialized gauge solutions for unique inspection requirements. Each designed for specific applications where standard gauges aren't sufficient.

Form Gauges

Complex contour verification for profiles, radii, and irregular shapes

Fixture Gauges

Multi-feature inspection gauges for simultaneous dimensional verification

Attribute Gauges

GO/NO-GO gauges for multiple features with clear pass/fail indication

Taper Gauges

Precision taper verification for Morse, Jacobs, and custom taper forms

Spline Gauges

Internal and external spline gauges for involute and straight-sided splines

Custom Gauge Design & Manufacturing

Engineered for Your Application

When standard gauges won't work, our engineering team designs and manufactures custom solutions tailored to your exact requirements. From concept through production, we ensure your custom gauge delivers the accuracy and reliability you need.

Our Custom Gauge Process

  1. Requirement Analysis - We review your prints, specifications, and application to fully understand the inspection challenge.
  2. Design Development - Our engineers create detailed gauge designs with 3D models and GD&T specifications.
  3. Design Review - We review the design with you to ensure it meets all requirements before manufacturing begins.
  4. Manufacturing - Precision machining and grinding to exacting tolerances in our climate-controlled facility.
  5. Inspection & Certification - Complete dimensional verification with full documentation and traceability.

Custom Gauge Capabilities

Complex Geometries

Multi-axis CNC machining for intricate forms and features

Material Selection

Tool steel, carbide, ceramics, or specialized alloys

Tight Tolerances

Routinely hold ±0.000020" (±0.5 microns) or better

Reverse Engineering

Recreate existing gauges or improve upon current designs

Discuss Your Custom Gauge Project

Need Help Selecting the Right Gauge?

Our application engineers are available to help you choose the optimal gauge solution for your specific requirements. Contact us for technical assistance or a quote.
